---
title: "Robert J. McGrath"
subtitle: "Associate Professor, Schar School of Policy and Government, George Mason University"
image: images/profile.jpg
image-shape: rectangle
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:rmcgrat2@gmu.edu
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=kQEBg6wAAAAJ
    - icon: person-badge-fill
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0001-8963-3231
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/rjm328
---

<div class="director-line">Director of PhD Programs in Political Science &amp; Public Policy</div>

Robert J. McGrath earned his PhD in political science from the University of Iowa and holds a BA from Saint Peter's College. He was a Robert Wood Johnson Foundation Scholar in Health Policy Research at the University of Michigan from 2013 to 2015.

## Research Interests

His research spans American politics, legislative institutions, public administration, health policy, and federalism. He studies state-level political dynamics, policy spillovers, and the opioid crisis, with a focus on how political institutions shape policy outcomes across levels of government. His work examines how bureaucratic and legislative actors interact in the policy process, particularly in areas of regulatory implementation and oversight. He is also interested in how federal and state governments respond to public health crises and the role of intergovernmental dynamics in shaping those responses.
